hoses: Hydrant supply hose from hydrant to Engine 181 intake
FDC supply line A: 100 ft of 2.5-inch hose from Engine 181 discharge to hotel FDC inlet A
FDC supply line B: 100 ft of 2.5-inch hose from Engine 181 discharge to hotel FDC inlet B
Interior attack line A: 150 ft of 2.5-inch hose from 8th-floor standpipe outlet to fire room
Interior attack line B: 150 ft of 2.5-inch hose from 8th-floor standpipe outlet to fire room
nozzles: Interior attack line A with 1 1/8-inch smooth bore nozzle, 265 GPM @ 50 PSI
Interior attack line B with 1 1/8-inch smooth bore nozzle, 265 GPM @ 50 PSI
appliances: Hotel FDC on exterior wall near main entrance
sceneElements.elevation: Fire room is on the 8th floor, requiring 35 PSI elevation pressure

problems.title: Total Standpipe Flow
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying two interior standpipe attack lines. Each line flows 265 GPM. What is the total flow for this standpipe operation?
formulaBreakdown: Total flow = attack line A flow + attack line B flow.
problems.instructorExplanation: The engine is supplying two interior standpipe attack lines. Add both attack line flows to get the total system flow of 530 GPM.
problems.explainMistake: Do not count only one attack line. Both interior lines are supplied through the standpipe system.

problems.title: Flow Per FDC Supply Line
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying the hotel FDC with two equal supply lines. The total standpipe flow is 530 GPM. How much flow is carried by each FDC supply line?
formulaBreakdown: Flow per FDC line = total standpipe flow / number of FDC supply lines.
problems.instructorExplanation: The two FDC supply lines split the total standpipe flow equally. Each FDC supply line carries 265 GPM.
problems.explainMistake: Do not calculate each FDC supply line using the full 530 GPM. The two supply lines share the flow.

problems.title: FDC Supply Line Friction Loss
problems.question: Each FDC supply line is 100 ft of 2.5-inch hose flowing 265 GPM. What is the friction loss in each FDC supply line?
formulaBreakdown: FL = C x (GPM / 100)^2 x hose length in hundreds.
problems.instructorExplanation: Each FDC supply line carries 265 GPM. At 265 GPM through 100 ft of 2.5-inch hose, friction loss rounds to 15 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not use the total 530 GPM in one FDC supply line. Each line carries half of the total flow.

problems.title: Standpipe Elevation Pressure
problems.question: The fire room is on the 8th floor. Using 5 PSI per floor above the 1st floor, what elevation pressure must be added?
formulaBreakdown: Elevation pressure = floors above 1st floor x 5 PSI.
8th floor is 7 floors above the 1st floor.
problems.instructorExplanation: The 8th floor is seven floors above the 1st floor. At 5 PSI per floor, the standpipe operation requires 35 PSI for elevation.
problems.explainMistake: Do not add 5 PSI for the 1st floor. The 1st floor is the baseline elevation.

problems.title: Standpipe Pump Pressure
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying the hotel FDC with two equal 100 ft 2.5-inch FDC supply lines. Total standpipe flow is 530 GPM, so each FDC supply line carries 265 GPM. Each interior attack line is 150 ft of 2.5-inch hose flowing 265 GPM through a 1⅛-inch smooth bore nozzle at 50 PSI. The fire is on the 8th floor, adding 35 PSI elevation. Add 25 PSI standpipe/FDC system loss. What pump discharge pressure should Engine 181 set for one complete standpipe flow path?
formulaBreakdown: PP = FL + NP + elevation + appliance loss.
Friction loss = FDC supply line FL + interior attack line FL.
problems.instructorExplanation: For this standpipe operation, calculate one complete supply path from Engine 181 to the FDC, through the standpipe, and through the interior attack line. Add the FDC supply line friction loss, interior hose friction loss, nozzle pressure, elevation, and standpipe/FDC loss. The required pump pressure is 145 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not add friction loss for both equal FDC supply lines together. Parallel supply lines split the flow, and one complete supply path governs the pump pressure.

problems.title: FDC Supply Hose Count
problems.question: How many hoses from Engine 181 are connected directly to the hotel FDC?
formulaBreakdown: The scene shows two hoses from Engine 181 to the hotel FDC.
problems.instructorExplanation: The hydrant supplies Engine 181, and Engine 181 supplies the hotel FDC with two separate hose lines.
problems.explainMistake: Do not count the hydrant supply hose as an FDC supply hose. The FDC is supplied by the two hoses from Engine 181.
